content: GramJS StringSession is the recommended persistence mechanism: call client.session.save() after first interactive login to get a string, then reuse via new StringSession(savedString). A StringSession grants full account access and effectively does not expire on its own (only revoked by logout/password change/Telegram action), so it must be treated as a top-tier secret: load from env/secret manager, never commit. If it is missing/invalid the client falls back to interactive phone+code login which cannot run unattended in Docker, causing a startup hang/crash. Rotate a leaked session by terminating the session in Telegram settings and regenerating.
